name: Fargo Va
description_short: Fargo VA Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the Fargo community with loans, credit cards, and deposit accounts.
description_long:
Fargo VA Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union operating in Fargo, North Dakota. The institution emphasizes member-first principles and community focus, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks by prioritizing member needs over profit maximization. As a credit union, it is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and operates on a cooperative model where members are owners rather than customers.

The credit union offers a range of financial products including home equity loans, share-secured loans, VISA credit cards, online banking, and ATM access. They provide competitive rates on select products, such as 6.00% APR on home equity loans and 11.50% APR on VISA credit cards as of January 1, 2026. Members can access no-fee ATMs and utilize services like Liberty Checks. The institution supports both individual members and businesses seeking to achieve financial stability and growth.

Fargo VA distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community investment and member prosperity. The credit union actively promotes financial education and planning to help members maintain long-term stability. Their accessibility features, including AudioEye optimization, demonstrate a commitment to serving diverse members. The organization positions itself as part of the local community rather than a distant corporate entity.

As a smaller regional credit union, Fargo VA may have more limited product offerings and technology capabilities compared to national banks or larger credit unions. Membership eligibility may be restricted to those living or working in the Fargo area. While the advertised rates are competitive, actual approval and rates depend on individual creditworthiness. The credit union's online presence and digital capabilities appear more basic than larger financial institutions.
pros: Member-owned cooperative model means profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
No-fee ATM network access for cost-effective cash withdrawals
Competitive home equity loan rates at 6.00% APR (as of 1/1/2026)
Multiple contact methods including phone, email, fax, and in-person visits for member support
Share-secured loans available at 2.50% APR for members with savings
Online banking and digital account access available to members
AudioEye accessibility features to serve members with disabilities
cons: Geographic limitations likely restrict membership to Fargo area residents or employees
Limited product range compared to larger national financial institutions
Advertised rates are promotional; actual rates depend on creditworthiness and current conditions
Website appears to lack detailed product information, fee schedules, and eligibility requirements
Smaller institution may have fewer branches and ATM locations than major banks
best_for: Fargo, North Dakota area residents seeking member-focused banking with local decision-making
Borrowers qualified for home equity or share-secured loans wanting competitive rates
Individuals preferring cooperative ownership model over traditional for-profit banking
Community-minded members valuing local reinvestment over corporate profit extraction
diagnosis: Fargo VA Federal Credit Union is best for residents of the Fargo area seeking community-focused banking with member ownership benefits and competitive loan rates. The main caveat is that membership eligibility may be geographically restricted, and the institution offers a more limited product selection compared to larger national banks or credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Fargo Va — NCUA-insured credit union in FARGO, ND. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
